# Spacing System
<!-- nib-foundation: spacing-system | generated: 2026-03-02 | do not edit manually -->

## Base Unit

All spacing is a multiple of **4px**. This is the fundamental unit of the layout grid.

Why 4px?
- Aligns with most platform grid systems (8pt on mobile = 2 × 4px)
- Results in clean pixel values at all common device densities
- Matches the line height grid for vertical rhythm consistency

---

## Scale

| Token | Value | Semantic alias |
|-------|-------|----------------|
| `spacing.0` | 0px | `spacing.none` |
| `spacing.4` | 4px | — |
| `spacing.8` | 8px | `spacing.2xs` |
| `spacing.12` | 12px | `spacing.xs` |
| `spacing.16` | 16px | `spacing.sm` |
| `spacing.20` | 20px | — |
| `spacing.24` | 24px | `spacing.md` |
| `spacing.32` | 32px | `spacing.lg` |
| `spacing.40` | 40px | — |
| `spacing.48` | 48px | `spacing.xl` |
| `spacing.64` | 64px | `spacing.2xl` |
| `spacing.80` | 80px | — |
| `spacing.96` | 96px | `spacing.3xl` |

> Always prefer semantic aliases (`spacing.sm`) over numeric values (`spacing.16`) in component code.
> Use numeric values only in layout utilities where the number is self-documenting.

---

## Layout Spacing vs Component Spacing

### Component Spacing (internal padding, gap between elements)

| Scenario | Token | Reason |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Icon gap in button | `spacing.xs` (8px) | Tight; icon and label are visually unified |
| Input padding (vertical) | `spacing.sm` (12px) | Standard touch target height |
| Input padding (horizontal) | `spacing.md` (16px) | Comfortable text breathing room |
| Card internal padding | `spacing.lg` (24px) | Content has room without feeling sparse |
| Dialog padding | `spacing.xl` (32px) | Dialog content needs more air |

### Layout Spacing (between sections, regions)

| Scenario | Token | Reason |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Sibling form fields | `spacing.md` (16px) | Close relationship, easy to scan |
| Card grid gap | `spacing.lg` (24px) | Cards are related but distinct |
| Section vertical rhythm | `spacing.2xl` (48px) | Clear separation between content sections |
| Hero to content gap | `spacing.3xl` (64px) | Major transition on the page |

---

## Negative Space Rules

- **Padding** > **margin** by default. Padding is contained; margin can collapse.
- Use **gap** on flex/grid containers rather than margin on children.
- For list items: use `spacing.xs` gap for dense lists, `spacing.sm` for comfortable.
- Avoid spacing above the first element and below the last element in a container — use container padding instead.

---

## Density Modes (Coming in Phase 5)

The token system is designed to support density modes:
- **Compact** — multiply all component spacing by ~0.75
- **Comfortable** — default (current)
- **Spacious** — multiply all component spacing by ~1.5

This will be implemented via CSS custom property overrides on a root class. No code changes required in components.
